service.description: Handle MCP initialize request using Moqui authentication
Handle MCP tools/call request with direct Moqui service execution
Handle MCP resources/list request with Moqui entity discovery based on user permissions
Handle MCP resources/read request with Moqui entity queries
Handle MCP ping request for health check
Debug service to verify component is loaded and working

actions.script:
import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext import org.moqui.impl.context.UserFacadeImpl.UserInfo
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
def status = [ componentLoaded: true, componentName: "mo-mcp", timestamp: ec.user.getNowTimestamp(), user: ec.user.username, userId: ec.user.userId, serviceNames: ec.service.getKnownServiceNames().findAll { it.contains("Mcp") }, entityNames: ec.entity.getAllEntityNames().findAll { it.contains("ArtifactHit") } ]
ec.logger.info("=== MCP COMPONENT DEBUG ===") ec.logger.info("Component status: ${status}") ec.logger.info("All service names count: ${ec.service.getKnownServiceNames().size()}") ec.logger.info("All entity names count: ${ec.entity.getAllEntityNames().size()}") ec.logger.info("=== END MCP COMPONENT DEBUG ===")
result.status = status

import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
// For now, return empty templates list - can be extended later def templates = []
result = [resourceTemplates: templates]

import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
ec.logger.info("Resource subscription requested for URI: ${uri}, sessionId: ${sessionId}")
// For now, just return success - actual subscription tracking could be added result = [subscribed: true, uri: uri]

import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
ec.logger.info("Resource unsubscription requested for URI: ${uri}, sessionId: ${sessionId}")
// For now, just return success - actual subscription tracking could be added result = [unsubscribed: true, uri: uri]

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Values for the prompt template placeholders

actions.script:
import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
// For now, return empty roots list - can be extended later def roots = []
result = [roots: roots]

import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
ec.logger.info("Sampling createMessage requested for sessionId: ${sessionId}")
// For now, return not implemented - can be extended with actual LLM integration result = [error: "Sampling not implemented"]

import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
ec.logger.info("Elicitation create requested for sessionId: ${sessionId}")
// For now, return not implemented - can be extended later result = [error: "Elicitation not implemented"]

service.description: Read-only (GET) proxy to the Moqui REST API for data inspection / debugging. Loops back to /rest/{path} forwarding the inbound Basic auth credential. Restricted to the SystemSupport account (configurable via moqui.mcp.rest.allowed.username). Covers the Service REST API (s1/moqui, s1/mantle), the Entity/Master REST API (e1/{Entity}, m1/{Entity}/{master}) and the schema/swagger discovery endpoints. No mutating verbs are ever issued.

parameter.name: path
parameter.description: REST path relative to /rest/ (leading '/' or 'rest/' is stripped), e.g. 's1/mantle/products', 'e1/mantle.party.Party/100000', 'service.swagger/moqui.json'.

service.description: List MCP tools: service discovery/execution, read-only REST, knowledge and prompts.

actions.script:
import org.moqui.context.ExecutionContext
ExecutionContext ec = context.ec
def tools = [ [ name: "moqui_prompts_list", title: "List Prompts", description: "List available MCP prompt templates.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [:] ] ], [ name: "moqui_prompts_get", title: "Get Prompt", description: "Retrieve and render a specific MCP prompt template (documentation/guidance text, NOT a service). Call moqui_prompts_list first to see valid names. To find or run business logic use moqui_search_services / moqui_execute_service.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "name": [type: "string", description: "Prompt name exactly as returned by moqui_prompts_list. Not a service name — never pass verb#Noun here."], "arguments": [type: "object", description: "Arguments for prompt template"] ], required: ["name"] ] ], [ name: "moqui_search_services", title: "Search Services", description: "Search for Moqui services by name, noun, or verb. Returns each match with its description, required parameters, and optional parameters. Use this to answer questions like 'list services', 'what services exist for Product', or 'how do I update a product'. Leave query broad (e.g. 'product', 'order', 'party') to see all related services.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "query": [type: "string", description: "Search term (e.g. 'product', 'update#growerp', 'create#mantle'). Use a single keyword to browse a domain."] ], required: ["query"] ] ], [ name: "moqui_get_service_details", title: "Get Service Details", description: "Get full input and output parameter details for a specific Moqui service, including types and descriptions. Use after moqui_search_services to see all parameters before executing.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "serviceName": [type: "string", description: "Fully-qualified service name in the form package.path.ServiceFile.verb#Noun (e.g. 'growerp.100.CatalogServices100.get#Products'). Get the exact name from moqui_search_services — do not guess."] ], required: ["serviceName"] ] ], [ name: "moqui_execute_service", title: "Execute Service", description: "Execute a Moqui service directly with parameters. Use moqui_search_services first to find the service name, then moqui_get_service_details to learn required parameters, then call this to run it.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "serviceName": [type: "string", description: "Fully-qualified service name in the form package.path.ServiceFile.verb#Noun (e.g. 'growerp.100.CatalogServices100.get#Products'). Get the exact name from moqui_search_services — do not guess."], "parameters": [type: "object", description: "Input parameters for the service (required params must be provided)"] ], required: ["serviceName"] ] ], [ name: "moqui_rest_call", title: "Moqui REST API (read-only)", description: "Read-only (GET) call to the Moqui REST API for debugging and data inspection. 'path' is relative to /rest/ — e.g. 's1/moqui/...' (Moqui Tools API), 's1/mantle/...' (Mantle USL API), 'e1/{Entity}/{id}' and 'm1/{Entity}/{master}/{id}' (entity / master-entity API, any entity by name). Filter/page with queryParameters (e.g. {pageSize:'5', statusId:'...', orderByField:'...'}). Discover endpoints/schemas via 'service.swagger/moqui.json', 'service.swagger/mantle.json', 'entity.swagger', 'master.swagger'. GET only — no create/update/delete. Restricted to the SystemSupport account.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "path": [type: "string", description: "REST path relative to /rest/ (e.g. 'e1/mantle.party.Party/100000', 's1/mantle/products', 'service.swagger/moqui.json')"], "queryParameters": [type: "object", description: "Optional query/filter parameters appended to the request (e.g. {pageSize:'10', orderByField:'lastName'})"] ], required: ["path"] ] ], [ name: "searchKnowledge", title: "Search Company Knowledge", description: "Search THIS company's knowledge base (uploaded documents, policies, notes) for passages relevant to a question. Use it for company-specific information that is not in the live ERP data — policies, procedures, product descriptions. Returns the most relevant passages; cite them and do not invent facts not present.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "query": [type: "string", description: "The question or topic to find relevant company knowledge for"], "limit": [type: "integer", description: "Max passages to return (default 5)"] ], required: ["query"] ] ], [ name: "okf_index", title: "OKF Bundle Index", description: "Return the index of the curated OKF knowledge bundle (stable domain knowledge: entity/data-model definitions, schemas, relationships). Start here to see which concepts exist, then load one with okf_load_concept. Prefer OKF over searchKnowledge for data-model/domain-structure questions.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "wikiSpaceId": [type: "string", description: "OKF bundle wiki space (default GROWERP_OKF)"] ] ] ], [ name: "okf_load_concept", title: "Load OKF Concept", description: "Load one concept of the OKF knowledge bundle: a markdown file with YAML frontmatter, e.g. an entity definition with its # Schema table and # Relationships links. Use the bundle-relative path from okf_index or okf_follow, e.g. 'tables/OrderHeader.md'. Load only the concepts you need (progressive disclosure).", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "path": [type: "string", description: "Bundle-relative concept path, e.g. 'tables/OrderHeader.md'"], "wikiSpaceId": [type: "string", description: "OKF bundle wiki space (default GROWERP_OKF)"] ], required: ["path"] ] ], [ name: "okf_follow", title: "Follow OKF Concept Links", description: "List the concepts linked from one OKF concept (its graph neighbours, e.g. related entities), so you can navigate the knowledge graph without loading whole documents. Broken links are reported, not errors.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "path": [type: "string", description: "Bundle-relative concept path whose links to resolve, e.g. 'tables/OrderHeader.md'"], "wikiSpaceId": [type: "string", description: "OKF bundle wiki space (default GROWERP_OKF)"] ], required: ["path"] ] ], [ name: "moqui_get_help", title: "Get Help Documentation", description: "Fetch extended wiki documentation for a service ('wiki:service:<Name>') or a multi-step business process workflow ('wiki:workflow:<Name>', e.g. 'wiki:workflow:Order-Entry'). Workflow docs give step-by-step tool calls for complex operations.", inputSchema: [ type: "object", properties: [ "uri": [type: "string", description: "Help URI: 'wiki:service:<Name>' or 'wiki:workflow:<Name>'"] ], required: ["uri"] ] ] ]
result = [tools: tools]

service.description: Fetch extended wiki documentation for a service or business process workflow.
